NCT.evidence: Found in the 'Trial registration' section on page 2: 'ClinicalTrials.gov identifier NCT00732914'.

PubMed ID.evidence: PubMed ID was not explicitly reported in the document. A DOI was provided instead.

Author.evidence: Extracted the first author listed on page 1 under the title of the article.

Year.evidence: Found in the header on page 1: 'EUROPEAN UROLOGY 68 (2015)837-847'.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document contains extensive sections including abstract, introduction, methods, results, discussion, and references, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Found in the abstract on page 1 under 'Design, setting, and participants': 'phase 3 SWITCH study'. Also confirmed on page 2 under '2.1. Study design and patients': 'phase 3 study'.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The study is described as 'A Randomised, Sequential, Open-label Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Sorafenib-sunitinib Versus Sunitinib-sorafenib' in the title and abstract, indicating it is the original report of this specific trial.

Number of Arms.evidence: Found in the abstract on page 1 under 'Intervention': 'Patients were randomised to sorafenib... followed by sunitinib... (So-Su), or vice versa (Su-So)', clearly indicating two arms.

Treatment Arm.evidence: Found in the abstract on page 1 under 'Intervention': 'Patients were randomised to sorafenib 400 mg twice daily followed... by sunitinib 50 mg once daily... (So-Su)'.

Treatment Arm 2.evidence: Found in the abstract on page 1 under 'Intervention': 'Patients were randomised to sorafenib... followed by sunitinib... (So-Su), or vice versa (Su-So)'.

Control Arm.evidence: This study compares two active sequential treatment arms (So-Su vs Su-So) rather than a treatment arm against a placebo or standard control arm.

Type of Agent in Treatment Arm.evidence: Found in the abstract on page 1: 'multikinase inhibitors sorafenib followed by sunitinib'. Also on page 2 under '1. Introduction': 'Sorafenib and sunitinib are multikinase inhibitors'. The discussion section on page 5 also refers to 'sequential tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) therapy'.

Is study included in Meta-analysis.evidence: The document describes "SWITCH" as a "Randomised, Sequential, Open-label Study" and a "prospective, randomised, phase 3 study" in the title and abstract, indicating it is a primary study, not a meta-analysis.

Is OS data available.evidence: The abstract states "Secondary endpoints included overall survival (OS) and safety." The results section on page 2 reports "OS was similar for So-Su and Su-So (median 31.5 and 30.2 mo; HR 1.00, 90% CI 0.77–1.30; p = 0.5 for superiority)." Figure 2B also presents Kaplan-Meier plots for overall survival.

Is QoL data available.evidence: The abstract and the "Study endpoints and assessments" section (page 2) list the primary and secondary endpoints, which include progression-free survival, overall survival, and safety, but do not mention quality of life (QoL) data. While a reference [19] mentions "Q-TWIST analyses" in an updated analysis, this specific document does not present QoL data as part of its reported findings.

The number of arms.evidence: The abstract's "Intervention" section states, "Patients were randomised to sorafenib 400 mg twice daily followed... by sunitinib... (So-Su), or vice versa (Su-So)," clearly describing two treatment arms. The results section on page 3 also confirms "365 patients were randomised (182 to So-Su; 183 to Su-So)".

Risk.value: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center (MSKCC) risk score
Risk.evidence: The abstract states the study was "stratified by Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center risk score (favourable or intermediate)." Table 1 also presents "MSKCC risk score" data for the patient population.

N | Treatment Arm.evidence: Found in the 'Results and limitations' section on page 2: 'In total, 365 patients were randomised (So-Su, n = 182; Su-So, n = 183).'

N | Control Arm.evidence: Found in the 'Results and limitations' section on page 2: 'In total, 365 patients were randomised (So-Su, n = 182; Su-So, n = 183).'

Median Follow Up Duration (mo).evidence: The document mentions 'mean follow-up' (10.3 mo on page 3) and 'median follow-up for patients without an OS event' (1.4 mo for So-Su and 3.0 mo for Su-So on page 4), but a general 'Median Follow Up Duration (mo)' for the entire study population is not explicitly reported.

Sex | Males(%).evidence: Extracted from Table 1, 'Sex, n (%)', row 'Male', column 'Total (n = 365)': '274 (75)'.

Sex | Females(%).evidence: Extracted from Table 1, 'Sex, n (%)', row 'Female', column 'Total (n = 365)': '91 (25)'.

Favorable Risk PFS | Rx (mo).evidence: The document states that "Median total PFS and median first-line PFS were generally similar between the treatment arms across patient subgroups categorised according to age, sex, ECOG PS, and MSKCC score" in section 3.4. Figure 4 provides Hazard Ratios for PFS by MSKCC risk score but does not provide specific median PFS values for the Favorable Risk subgroup for either treatment arm.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: improvement in progression-free survival (PFS) with So-Su versus Su-So, assessed from randomisation to progression or death during second-line therapy
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The primary endpoint is stated in the 'Outcome measurements and statistical analysis' section on page 1 and confirmed in '2.2. Study endpoints and assessments' on page 2.

Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The secondary endpoints are stated in the 'Outcome measurements and statistical analysis' section on page 1 and confirmed in '2.2. Study endpoints and assessments' on page 2, and further detailed on page 3 under 'Secondary endpoints included overall survival (OS) and safety'.

OS (mo) | Rx.evidence: Found in the 'Results and limitations' section on page 2: 'OS was similar for So-Su and Su-So (median 31.5 and 30.2 mo...)' and confirmed in '3.2. Efficacy' on page 4: 'OS was similar in both arms (median 31.5 mo for So-Su and 30.2 mo for Su-So...)' and Figure 2B on page 6.

PFS (mo) | Rx.evidence: Found in the 'Results and limitations' section on page 2: 'no significant difference in total PFS between So-Su and Su-So (median 12.5 vs 14.9 mo...)' and confirmed in '3.2. Efficacy' on page 4: 'So-Su was not superior to Su-So in terms of PFS (median 12.5 vs 14.9 mo...)' and Figure 2A on page 6.

ORR (%) Rx | Overall.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Tumour response, n (%)' for 'First-line therapy' under 'So-Su (n=177)', 'Objective response rate b' is 55 (31%). Also mentioned on page 6: 'ORR (31% and 29%) ... were similar for first-line sorafenib and sunitinib, respectively.'

ORR (%) Control | CR.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Tumour response, n (%)' for 'First-line therapy' under 'Su-So (n=176)', 'Complete response' is 6 (3.4%).

TRAE Grade 5 (%) | Rx.evidence: Found in Table 3, 'Safety overview' under 'First-line So (n=177)', 'AEs related to deaths, n (%)' is 12 (6.7%). Grade 5 AEs are typically deaths.

TRAE Grade 5 (%) | Control.evidence: Found in Table 3, 'Safety overview' under 'First-line Su (n=176)', 'AEs related to deaths, n (%)' is 16 (9.1%). Grade 5 AEs are typically deaths.

No of Deaths | Rx.evidence: Found on page 3, 'The most frequent reasons for not initiating protocol-defined second-line treatment after stopping first-line treatment were death (So-Su n = 16 [8.8%]; Su-So n = 19 [10%])'. This refers to deaths before second-line treatment. Also confirmed in Figure 1 on page 4, under '58 (32%) not treated in 2nd-line', '16 died' for So-Su.

No of Deaths | Control.evidence: Found on page 3, 'The most frequent reasons for not initiating protocol-defined second-line treatment after stopping first-line treatment were death (So-Su n = 16 [8.8%]; Su-So n = 19 [10%])'. This refers to deaths before second-line treatment. Also confirmed in Figure 1 on page 4, under '80 (44%) not treated in 2nd-line', '19 died' for Su-So.

Significant QoL Benefit for Rx.evidence: The document does not report on Quality of Life (QoL) benefit for the Rx arm.
